name: cursor-cli
manual_instructions: Cursor CLI (`cursor-agent`) is a different binary from the editor launcher `cursor`. Both authenticate against the same Cursor account, so account-level Privacy Mode covers BOTH — set once in the editor (Cmd+Shift+J → Privacy → Privacy Mode) or at https://cursor.com/settings → Privacy. No CLI-specific toggle exists; even with your own API key, requests still route through Cursor's backend.
Business-plan accounts have Privacy Mode forced on (zero retention) — no action needed there.
`~/.cursor/cli-config.json` holds preferences only (vim mode, default model, shell-permissions allow/deny). No telemetry key — do not invent one.

process_check.if_running: cursor-agent is running. No settings edits apply (manual_only), but quit any interactive session before changing Cursor account Privacy Mode so the CLI re-reads the setting on next login.

persistent_files.note: CLI preferences + shell-permissions allow/deny (not telemetry; review manually).
Shared with editor — AI telemetry queue.

notes: Installed via `brew install --cask cursor-cli` (ships `cursor-agent`) or `curl https://cursor.com/install -fsS | bash`.

Bundled `merkle-tree-napi.darwin-arm64.node` is unsigned — first run trips macOS Gatekeeper. Known release-hygiene issue, not malware. Fix: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Allow Anyway, or scoped `xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/cursor-cli/`.
